scenario: S4 golden defect set (live: playground/home-redesign/src, coded fidelity)
evidence: Answer key: 9 real pre-existing defects enumerated independently against AGENTS.md forbidden patterns before running the procedure — K1 components/ChatSimulationCard.scss (39 hex lines, 0 var() tokens, FP-1, blocker); K2 demo.css:7-12 + index.css:6-11 (custom --chat-* palette from raw grays instead of DS tokens, FP-1, major); K3 components/Dashboard.jsx:45-48,63-64,77 (hardcoded chart hexes, inconsistent with tokenized lines 57-62, FP-1, major); K4 components/DevIndexPage.jsx (14 hex hits + pervasive inline px fontSize/margin, FP-1, major); K5 components/ChatSimulationCard.jsx:22,90,193 (raw <button> while DS Button is imported at line 3 and used in sibling files, FP-2/FP-6, major); K6 ChatSimulationCard.jsx:80 (raw <input>, DS forms cheat-sheet exists at skills/uno-prototype/references/cheat-forms.md, FP-2, major); K7 ChatSimulationCard.jsx:193 close-btn '×' + :90-98 icon-only SVG send button, no accessible name (WCAG 4.1.2, a11y, major); K8 ChatSimulationCard.jsx:8-15 VideoThumbnail play control is a non-interactive <div> — not keyboard reachable, no role/label (WCAG 2.1.1, a11y, major); K9 Dashboard.jsx:262 + MonthlyReportsListPage.jsx:74 (inline hardcoded px, FP-1, minor). PROCEDURE RESULT — scripts/run-review-checks.sh CRASHES after its first check: pass()/warn()/fail() use post-increment '(( PASS++ ))' (run-review-checks.sh:28-30) which returns exit status 1 when the counter is 0, and 'set -euo pipefail' (line 14) aborts the script — as shipped it can NEVER complete more than one check on any target (verified live: emitted only the hex WARN, exit 1, no STATUS line).
notes: Recall/precision, honestly layered: (a) script-as-shipped = 4/9 recall (44%) — surfaces only hex evidence (K1-K4), FAILS the >=80% bar; (b) manual catch-patterns.md greps run individually (the documented fallback) = 7/9 (78%) — adds K5, K6, K9; still under bar; (c) full procedure incl. a11y-lens checklist at coded depth (labels, keyboard reach, semantic HTML) = 9/9 (100%) — a11y-lens is what clears the bar, though its mandated 'inspect rendered output' (a11y-lens.md:20) is impossible read-only; source inspection sufficed. Precision: 85 raw hex hits include 20 non-defects (17 var(--token, #hex) fallbacks where the token IS used, 1 comment 'Changed from #fff' at ChatSimulationCard.scss:9, 2 pre-mount crash-screen literals in main.jsx:28/main.demo.jsx:34) → 76% line-level. Grouped-finding level: 10 true / 14 raw = 71% — under bar if grep hits are treated as verdicts; catch-patterns.md's own rule ('output is evidence, not a verdict') plus the FP-6 no-PLUS-equivalent exception (the script's react-bootstrap warn on main.jsx:3 ThemeProvider is a false positive — no PLUS ThemeProvider wrapper exists) lifts judged precision to 10/11 (91%). VERDICT AMBIGUOUS because the bar is met only by the manual/lens layers: the shipped automation is broken (44% recall) and catch-patterns.md documents exceptions only for comments and SVG fills — var() fallbacks and crash-screens rely on undocumented reviewer judgment. Minimal fixes: (1) run-review-checks.sh — replace '((X++))' with 'X=$((X+1))' in pass/warn/fail; (2) add 'var(--*, #hex) fallback values' and 'pre-mount error fallbacks' to catch-patterns.md exceptions; (3) scenario-file defect: S4 names 'N planted flaws per fidelity level' but no golden fixture exists anywhere under docs/evals/, N is undefined, and no answer-key location is given — recall/precision are unmeasurable as written; this run substituted pre-existing defects in playground/home-redesign/src as the key.

The recall/precision gate is DECLARED with an enforcing logger but its measurement instruments are degraded: (1) scripts/run-review-checks.sh:28-30 counter bug aborts after one check under set -e (see S4) — the mechanical evidence layer behind ds-lens is 44%-recall as shipped; (2) no golden defect fixture exists under docs/evals/ despite S4 and the rubric benchmark both invoking it; (3) design_qa_drift_catch target has no baseline yet (rubric says 'baseline first' — consistent, not a defect).
